search:spring+redis:spring boot @cacheab.content:
- Use `@Cacheable` to cache method results in Redis.
- Ensure Spring Boot version is up-to-date, e.g., 3.x for latest features.
- Configure `RedisTemplate` with Lettuce client for non-reactive use.
- Set up Redis Sentinel for high availability.
- Avoid using `SENTINEL REPLICAS` command by ensuring user permissions.
- Use `@CacheEvict` to remove cache entries when data changes.
- Configure TTL (Time To Live) in Redis for automatic expiration of cached data.
- Monitor cache performance and adjust settings as needed.

- Use `mvn archetype:generate -DgroupId=com.example -DartifactId=spring-boot-cache-demo -DarchetypeArtifactId=maven-archetype-quickstart -DinteractiveMode=false` to create a new Spring Boot project.
- Add dependency for Spring Data Redis in your `pom.xml`: `<dependency><groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId><artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-redis</artifactId></dependency>`
- Configure Lettuce as the Redis client in `application.properties`: 
  ```properties
  spring.redis.client-type=lettuce
  spring.redis.host=localhost
  spring.redis.port=6379
  ```
- Use `@Cacheable` annotation on methods to enable caching.
- Ensure your Redis server is running and accessible at the specified host and port.
- For Redis Sentinel, configure additional properties:
  ```properties
  spring.redis.sentinel.master=mymaster
  spring.redis.sentinel.nodes=localhost:26379,localhost:26380,localhost:26381
  ```
- Use `@EnableCaching` annotation on a configuration class to enable caching support.
- Latest versions as of my last update are Spring Boot 3.1.4 and Lettuce 6.1.5.RELEASE.
